name: Open OnDemand Batch Connect
summary: Prepare reviewable Open OnDemand Batch Connect app templates.
description: This skill provides a conservative skeleton for Open OnDemand Batch Connect apps, including manifest, form, submit, template, validation, and site-review artifacts for Slurm-backed portal workflows.

tools.name: bash
tools.notes: Runs local skeleton validation and the placeholder launcher.

tools.name: find
tools.notes: Lists app skeleton files for review.

tools.name: grep
tools.notes: Finds placeholders and resource-manager-specific native arguments.

tools.name: Open OnDemand
tools.notes: Required only for site-owned deployment and pilot testing.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.notes: Used indirectly by Open OnDemand on Slurm-backed sites.

inputs.name: cluster_id
inputs.description: Public Open OnDemand cluster id or placeholder for the target Slurm cluster.

inputs.name: account
inputs.description: Site-approved charge account for development or pilot sessions.

inputs.name: queue
inputs.description: Short partition or queue approved for portal app tests.

inputs.name: launcher
inputs.description: Site-approved command that the Batch Connect session should start.

inputs.name: site_policy
inputs.description: Local Open OnDemand policy covering authentication, proxying, logging, cleanup, and scheduler limits.

outputs.name: app_skeleton
outputs.description: Reviewable Batch Connect app directory with manifest, form, submit, and template files.

outputs.name: review_notes
outputs.description: Validation output and site checklist for portal maintainers.

examples.title: Review a minimal Batch Connect app manifest
Review user-facing Batch Connect form fields
Review Slurm-backed Batch Connect submission settings
Review the placeholder Batch Connect launcher

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced app skeleton files.
Run local Batch Connect skeleton review checks.
After site review, copy the skeleton into an Open OnDemand development area, replace placeholders, run a short pilot session, and confirm logs, cleanup, and scheduler limits.
